appeal_letter:
Re: Appeal of Denial for MRI of the Cervical Spine, date of service 04/22/2026 (Prior Authorization)

Dear Cigna Appeals Reviewer,

I am writing to appeal the denial of coverage for an MRI of the Cervical Spine performed on 04/22/2026.

The denial letter states that coverage was denied because prior authorization was not obtained before the procedure was performed. The letter explains that, according to the plan documents, advanced imaging such as MRI, CT, and PET scans requires prior authorization from Cigna to confirm the service is medically appropriate, and that because the provider did not submit a prior authorization request before the date of service, the claim was denied.

I respectfully ask that this decision be reconsidered in light of the circumstances. The clinical record reflects that I experienced sudden, severe numbness and weakness in the left arm following a minor car accident. The emergency room physician ordered the MRI immediately in order to rule out a spinal cord injury. Given the urgent and emergent nature of these symptoms, there was no opportunity to wait for a standard prior authorization window before the imaging was performed.

I ask that Cigna re-review this claim taking into account the emergent clinical presentation, the emergency room physician's decision to order the MRI immediately to rule out a spinal cord injury, and the time-sensitive nature of the symptoms that did not allow for advance authorization. I request that the denial be overturned and that the claim for the cervical spine MRI performed on 04/22/2026 be approved.

Please let me know if any additional documentation would assist in your review.

Not legal or medical advice. Draft assistance only.

Sincerely,
[Member Name]
[Member ID]
[Contact Information]
missing_evidence_checklist: Emergency room records documenting the sudden onset of left arm numbness and weakness and the clinical decision to order the MRI
Provider or emergency physician statement explaining the emergent need and why advance authorization was not feasible
The MRI report and any related imaging findings
Documentation connecting the symptoms to the minor car accident
Plan documents describing prior authorization requirements and any exception for emergent or urgent services
Member identification number, plan information, and the denial reference number
